finding.summary: No clear problem. Healthy dynamics (12 dB crest factor, 8 LU LRA), proper headroom (true peak -5.5 dBFS), solid mono compatibility (correlation 0.91), balanced center image (balance +0.1 dB), and a 12 dB mid-to-side spread consistent with a tastefully wide clean guitar. Spectrum tilts ~6 dB/octave downward from 100 Hz to 4 kHz, but with only three 1/3-octave points the tonal picture is too sparse to call it wrong, and the track name 'Balanced Clean Guitar' suggests the tilt is intentional. No FX chain or routing anomalies.
finding.probable_cause: No defect attributable from the measured data; capture shows a well-behaved, lightly-processed (in this FX chain, none) clean guitar signal.
finding.confidence_reason: Capture isolation verified and silence fraction is low (0.05), so levels and dynamics are reliable. Tonal confidence is capped because only three 1/3-octave bands are reported and there is no FX chain to attribute findings to.

evidence_refs.description: 12.0 dB crest factor indicates healthy, uncompressed dynamics.
8.0 LU LRA is moderate, not crushed.
-5.5 dBFS true peak leaves adequate inter-sample headroom.
-20.0 LUFS is a reasonable single-track level for a clean guitar.
0.91 correlation is solid; no phase problem.
+0.1 dB L/R balance is effectively centered.
-18.0 dB mid RMS matches overall RMS, indicating mono-dominant content.
-30.0 dB side RMS, 12 dB below mid, is a moderate stereo width.
0.05 silence fraction means the capture is mostly signal, so level stats are trustworthy.
Empty FX chain; no processing to evaluate or bypass.
Empty sends; no parallel processing to evaluate.
Empty receives; no bus-fed content to evaluate.

proposal.reason: No clear problem is supported by this capture: dynamics, headroom, stereo image, and routing are all healthy, and the three-band spectrum is too sparse to justify a tonal change. No safe move is supported.
